channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Periyar Tiger Reserve Launches Initiative to Control Invasive African Catfish
item.description: A conservation initiative has been launched in the Periyar Tiger Reserve (PTR) to manage invasive African catfish, balancing ecological protection with community livelihoods. The project, involving Ke...

item.title: Biologist Names Sea Slug Species After Cape Verde Keeper
item.description: A new species of sea slug has been discovered and named 'Aldisa vozinha' in honor of Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha, who gained global recognition during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Biologist Jesus Ortea,...

item.title: Sam Hartman and Tyla Ochoa's Alleged Relationship: Unconfirmed Details
item.description: The article explores the speculated relationship between American football quarterback Sam Hartman and former Wake Forest soccer player Tyla Ochoa. Hartman first hinted at dating a Wake Forest soccer ...

item.title: Tour de France shortens stage nine due to heatwave safety measures
item.description: The Tour de France has decided to shorten stage nine by 30 km (18.64 miles) due to extreme heatwave conditions, according to organisers. The original 185.5 km route from Malemort to Ussel will now be ...

item.title: RCMP lay murder charges following fatal collision in Vanderhoof
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) have laid a murder charge following a severe motor vehicle collision in Vanderhoof, British Columbia. The incident, which occurred on July 11, 2026, involved a...

item.title: UNICEF and BARMM Launch Women and Children Protection Unit in Lanao del Sur
item.description: The Women and Children Protection Unit (WCPU) in Wao, Lanao del Sur, has officially opened, marking a significant step in addressing violence and trauma against women and children in the Bangsamoro re...

item.title: Mad Cool Festival 2026 Day Three: Highlights from Twenty One Pilots, Interpol, and Other Artists
item.description: The third day of the Mad Cool Festival 2026 in Madrid delivered a diverse lineup of performances, capturing the essence of the event's vibrant atmosphere. Highlights included Karen Dió's energetic fem...

item.title: Iran Rejects U.S. Talks Amid Trump's Threats Over Strait of Hormuz Disputes
item.description: Iran has rejected U.S. President Donald Trump's call for continued peace talks without a ceasefire, demanding Washington meet Tehran's conditions for resolving transit issues through the Strait of Hor...

item.title: Child Care Centers Key to Preventing Violence Against Children in Tanzania
item.description: Social welfare advocate Dk Gwajima emphasizes that child care centers are vital in preventing violence against children by providing structured environments for safety, supervision, and professional c...

item.title: India's migration patterns reshaping demographic balance through youth and education
item.description: The article discusses how India's migrant workforce, particularly young and educated individuals, is altering the country's demographic landscape. Researchers from Azim Premji University analyzed data...

item.title: 50 Years of Coastal Protection: Trump's Threat to California's Coastal Act
item.description: The article commemorates the 50th anniversary of California's Coastal Act, a landmark law that protected the state's coastline from overdevelopment. It highlights how President Trump and his administr...

item.title: Therafarm's Approach to Data Protection in Mental Health AI Platforms
item.description: The article discusses how Glory Okwata's Therafarm platform integrates artificial intelligence to address mental health challenges while prioritizing data protection. It highlights the unique risks of...

item.title: Sunday Superquiz edition published with access to additional crossword puzzles for premium subscribers
item.description: This article announces the Sunday, 12 July edition of the Superquiz, a regular puzzle feature written by Andrew Brooks and published for readers of The Sydney Morning Herald. Rather than reporting on ...

item.title: DPWH ensures all NCR pumping stations operational amid heavy rains
item.description: Despite heavy rainfall from Typhoon Inday and the southwest monsoon, the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) confirmed that all pumping stations in the National Capital Region (NCR) were fu...

item.title: Bandits abduct corpse and mourners, demand N450 ransom in Kogi
item.description: Bandits ambushed a vehicle carrying the remains of late Deputy Corps Commander Augustine O. Ikwue of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC), along with mourners, in Kogi State. The attack occurred on th...

item.title: CBS News Faces Crisis Under Bari Weiss as Ratings and Staff Plunge
item.description: A media expert has criticized CBS News for its declining performance under editor-in-chief Bari Weiss, citing historic low ratings and staff exodus. Oliver Darcy, a former CNN reporter, detailed the n...

item.title: UK Allocates £1.28m for Wildlife Conservation Projects in Kent
item.description: The UK government has awarded a £1.28 million grant to the Kent Wildlife Trust through Natural England's Species Recovery Programme, aimed at bolstering conservation efforts for endangered species suc...

item.title: England Fans Embrace Pint-Tossing Tradition at World Cup Celebrations
item.description: The phenomenon of fan parks has transformed how England supporters celebrate World Cup matches, with spontaneous pint-tossing celebrations becoming a hallmark of the experience. At venues like The Gar...

item.title: Alleged Forged Documents Link Ivanka Trump's Albanian Resort to Cocaine Money Laundering
item.description: A Miami businessman linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner's planned $1.6 billion luxury resort on Albania's Sazan Island is under investigation for allegedly using forged deeds to launder proceeds ...

item.title: Four Men Convicted for Armed Robberies Targeting Teachers in KZN
item.description: Four men have been convicted by the Pinetown Regional Court for a series of armed robberies targeting schools in KwaZulu-Natal, where teachers were robbed of their cellphones and forced to transfer mo...
